Novaterra Energy SE — Operational/Cyber–ESG Incident Note (EU, MAR/GDPR/CSRD context), internal circulation within EU group.
Summary: short SFTP outage at the Luserne District EU data hub during a scheduled vendor patch; production resumed within maintenance window; no customer systems affected.
Data: logs comprise pseudonymous device telemetry (e.g., DEV-AXX-001), no special categories per GDPR Art 9, and no employee identifiers beyond functional IDs; DPO review complete.
Transfer: mirroring to approved processors in Karsenia and Sagora under SCCs paused pending re-validation; TIA and SCC Annexes filed under Register Ref GRC-TR-114.
Security: forensics show no exfiltration; threat model severity Low; no Art 33 notification to the competent authority required under current criteria.
Disclosure: our public CSRD timetable (press note dated 15 Jan) remains in force; website statement confirms June release window for ESG KPIs; SFDR PAI data collection continues as planned.
Preliminary internal forecast indicates a not-yet-announced deferral of the interim ESG KPI annex by up to one week, which may interact with a sustainability-linked facility covenant currently in testing.
MAR: on present facts, we do not classify the above as inside information under EU MAR; blackout and no‑tipping reminders apply to the CSRD workstream until the next scheduled disclosure check.
Insider-handling: no insider list has been opened for this event; the template insider-list.csv and the information-barrier quick guide are provided for training and tabletop purposes only.
Crypto/ESG tech: the “EcoChain Trace” module and “GreenLedger Token” are marketing descriptors for a pilot traceability tool; there is no token issuance, listing, or commercial term change arising from this incident.
Market context: Novaterra Energy SE is admitted to trading on the Nordriver Exchange Market (EU MTF) and continues normal operations; no communication with analysts beyond public FAQs.
Commercial terms: vendor contract NEM-CT-2026 remains on standard SLAs; no renegotiation or price adjustment triggered by the outage.
Personal data minimisation: no natural-person names collected; role mailboxes only (e.g., dpo@novaterra.example, security@novaterra.example); data retention 30 days.
Contacts: stakeholders should route queries to compliance@novaterra.example or the public helpline listed on our website; do not forward internally beyond the incident channel.
Next steps: resume cross-border mirroring after SCC validation, confirm CSRD timeline at the next scheduled public update, and close the ticket if no further indicators arise.
